title: Multifunctional wound treatment dressing
abstract: The present invention relates to a multifunctional wound treatment dressing for administering a plurality of different therapeutic treatments to an open, chronic wound of a human or animal body. The proposed multifunctional wound treatment dressing comprises an occlusive wrapping (3) adapted for placing over a wound (2). The proposed multifunctional wound treatment dressing further comprises at least two of the group of electrical, optical or mechanical stimulation means adapted for transmitting electrical, optical or mechanical energy towards the wound to stimulate healing of the wound and/or means for applying a negative (i.e. sub-atmospheric) pressure to the wound (2). Thus, a variety of different healing stimulation treatments are possible performed together or in sequence by wearing the same occlusive wrapping (3) without the need of changing/removing temporarily the wrapping (3) for each change of treatment method.
claims:
Claims 1. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing (1), comprising an occlusive wrapping (3) adapted for placing over a wound (2) on the surface of a skin (1), one side of the wrapping (3) intended to face the wound (2) and comprising a connector (4) to be connected with an external apparatus box, further comprising a combination of at least two of the following means: - a negative pressure means, forming a substantially fluid- tight seal around the wound; and/or - an electrical stimulation means, comprising at least one electrode (12) arranged at the wound treatment dressing (1) and adapted for transmitting electrical energy towards the wound (2) and/or - an optical stimulation means, comprising an optical energy conducting and/or emitting element (16; 14) arranged at the wrapping (3) and adapted for transmitting optical energy towards the wound (2); and/or - a mechanical stimulation means, comprising a vibrational energy source (12), such as an ultrasound transducer, arranged at the wrapping (3) and adapted for transmitting vibrational energy towards the wound (2); and/or - a means for removing wound exudates to an exterior of the wrapping (2) . 2. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to claim 1, characterized in that the wrapping (3) is flexible or rigid or is partly flexible and partly rigid. 3. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the wrapping (3) is individually customized according to the specific shape of a body part of an individual, at which body part the wound is located. 4. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 3, characterized in that it further comprises a sensor (9) for determining a state or status of the wound (2), for instance for monitoring a variety of parameters such as pH, temperature, tissue closure, infection, biomarkers of healing, enzymes or moisture level. 5. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to claim 4, characterized in that the sensor (9) comprises an optical sensor adapted for illuminating the wound (2) and collecting information regarding the wound scattering parameters . 6. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 5, characterized in that it 

further comprises means for analyzing fluid exudate removed from the wound via a tube, in particular for identifying progress of wound healing, in particular adapted for determining one or more analytes indicative of one or more biochemical reactions that occur during wound recovery. 7. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 6, characterized in that it further comprises a control unit adapted for adjusting parameters in the wound treatment dressing and/or at the wound site, in particular dependent on data provided by the sensor . 8. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 7, characterized in that the electrical stimulation means is adapted for providing pulsed electrical stimulation, in particular is capable of delivering electrical pulses at a pre-selected or controllable pulse rate and intensity, and in particular is adjustable in response to changes in the determined state or status of the wound. 9. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to claim 8, characterized in that the electrical stimulation means comprises an array of electrodes (12), in particular the electrical stimulation means is adapted for creating at least one composite electrode from at least one of the electrodes (12) in the array, wherein in particular 

the electrode composition of the at least one composite electrode (12) is adjustable in response to changes in the determined state or status of the wound. 10. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 9, characterized in that the optical stimulation means is adapted to provide at least one of an infrared (IR), near-infrared (NIR) , ultraviolet (UV, in particular UVB) emission towards the wound. 11. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 10, characterized in that the optical stimulation means is adapted for providing pulsed optical stimulation, in particular is capable of delivering optical pulses at a pre-selected or controllable pulse rate and intensity. 12. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 11, characterized in that the optical energy conducting and/or emitting element is adapted for providing a selected light having substantially similar intensity across the wound site, in particular comprises a light conducting substrate (14) and at least one emitter mounted to emit light into the substrate. 13. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to claim 12, characterized in that the optical energy emitting element comprises a plurality of emitting sources 

 (16) arranged at spaced locations along at least a portion of a periphery of the wound site, or comprises at least one light emitting diode (LED) , or comprises at least one optical fiber (15), the optical fiber (15) for instance being split at one end to form a plurality of sub-fibers. 14. A multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to any one of claims 1 to 13, characterized in that it comprises an external apparatus box (5) and a connection arrangement (6), the connection arrangement (6) being adapted to receive one or more optical fibers (15) and/or cables (8) and/or wires (10) and/or suction tubes (13) and being further adapted for providing a connection to at least two of the following: - a pumping means for generating a sub-atmospheric pressure, e.g. a vacuum pump; - an electrical power source; - an optical source, such as an IR, NIR, UV (in particular UVB) source; - a control unit. 15. Method for a therapeutic wound treatment using a multifunctional wound treatment dressing according to one of claims 1 to 14 by forming a substantially fluid-tight seal with an occlusive wrapping (2) around the wound and by combining of at least two of the following methods: 

- applying a negative pressure around the wound (2) by the wrapping (2); and/or - transmitting electrical energy towards the wound (2) from the wrapping (2) and/or - transmitting optical energy towards the wound (2) from the wrapping (2); and/or - transmitting vibrational energy towards the wound (2) from the wrapping (2); and/or - removing wound exudates to an exterior of the wrapping (2) .
